title: FIX-467: Bug Creating First Library Ticket — Root Cause & Fix Walkthrough
description: This tour walks through the ticket history and code changes made to fix a bug where creating the first library ticket failed in the staging environment. The core issue was that the system was incorrectly using the user's authentication token instead of the built-in Helix system token when creating repositories — specifically for NetSuite file cabinet repos. Multiple runs were required to fully diagnose and address the root cause, as an earlier fix resolved a surface symptom but not the underlying token-selection logic.

steps.description: This is the original ticket filed by the user reporting that no artifacts were being produced when creating a new library ticket in the staging environment. The user noted that hours had passed without results, and attached screenshots as evidence. This ticket kicked off the multi-run investigation. Key context: the database available to the agent is not an exact copy of production, but the fix should ensure the new library is created regardless.
steps.title: Ticket FIX-467: Initial Report

steps.description: In this run, Helix acknowledged the ticket and began working on it. The discussion section shows the first agent response at 2026-05-15T00:13:39Z. No code changes were landed yet — this run was focused on understanding the failure mode from the attached screenshots and available database state. Data flow at this stage: user report → agent triage → environment inspection.
steps.title: Run 2: Agent Acknowledges and Begins Investigation

steps.description: By this run, Helix had delivered a first fix and posted verification screenshots. However, the user (Usher) replied at 2026-05-15T03:30:52Z explaining the issue persisted. Usher identified the real root cause: when creating a NetSuite file cabinet repository, the system was using the *user's* OAuth token rather than the built-in Helix system token. This is a critical distinction — the user token may not have the permissions needed to create repositories, while the system token does. Helix's response at 03:31:20Z confirms understanding of this exact problem, setting up the deeper fix in the next run.
steps.title: Run 3: First Fix Attempted — Partial Resolution

steps.description: This is the final and most complete run addressing FIX-467. The fix targets the token-selection logic: when the Helix agent creates the first library ticket and needs to initialize a repository (including NetSuite file cabinet repos), it must use the built-in Helix system token — not the authenticated user's token. This ensures that repository creation succeeds regardless of the individual user's permission scope. The full discussion history is preserved here, confirming the fix path: initial report → agent triage → partial fix → user feedback identifying root cause → correct fix applied using system token for repo creation.
steps.title: Run 4: Final Fix — System Token Used for Repo Creation

steps.description: With the fix in place, the corrected data flow for first-library-ticket creation is: (1) User triggers library ticket creation in the UI → (2) Helix backend receives the request → (3) System checks whether a repository needs to be initialized → (4) Repository creation uses the built-in Helix system token (NOT the user token) → (5) Artifact is created and linked to the ticket → (6) User sees artifacts in the UI. This path is now clean for all repo types including NetSuite file cabinet repos. The multi-run ticket history in `.helix/tickets/` serves as the audit trail confirming the root cause was properly identified and resolved.
steps.title: Summary: Data Flow After the Fix
